design_philosophy.core_loop: Select club and course position, aim shot accounting for wind and terrain, execute swing, observe result, progress through 18-hole rounds against 60 competitor AI
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing realistic golf physics (wind conditions, ball movement, terrain contours) with accessible gameplay for both golf enthusiasts and casual players
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Experience authentic PGA tournament competition on real and fictional courses with professional-level challenge and presentation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Prioritizes simulation fidelity and realism over arcade simplification; emphasizes multiple perspectives and preparation tools (3D hole views, overhead maps, contoured green grids) to reward planning and course study rather than pure reflexes

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Variable wind conditions create dynamic challenge; multiple game modes (Tournament, Driving, Putting, Practice) allow difficulty scaling; player competes against 60 unseen AI competitors with displayed statistics

progression.unlock_mechanism: Course progression through tournament rounds; access to different real courses (PGA West, TPC Avenel, TPC Sawgrass) and fictional Sterling Shores
replayability_hooks: Driving and Putting focused modes for skill refinement
Replay system for reviewing shots and studying technique

world_building_techniques: Licensed PGA Tour branding and real course venues
PGA golfer advice system for authenticity

environmental_storytelling: Real golf course environments (PGA West, TPC Avenel, TPC Sawgrass) establish authentic sporting context
3D hole visualization and contoured green grids communicate terrain topology
Overhead course maps provide strategic spatial information
Wind condition display indicates environmental hazards

sources_used: wikipedia: PGA Tour Golf gameplay section describing core mechanics, courses, modes, and environmental systems
wikipedia: Reception section documenting critical praise for realism, graphics, physics, sound, and camera systems
